title: Sort Trash and Recycling
summary: Learn which items go in the trash, recycling, or compost, and keep bins clean and organized.
learnerPromise: Sort waste correctly and keep bins clean.
whyItMatters: Sorting waste properly helps the environment, keeps your home tidy, and follows local rules. It also makes taking out the trash quicker and easier.
realLifeUses: Sorting items after cooking a meal
Breaking down cardboard boxes from deliveries
Knowing which bin to use for empty bottles and cans
Keeping the kitchen trash area from smelling
youWillLearn: How to read your local recycling guidelines
Which common items go in each bin
How to rinse containers before recycling
How to keep bins from smelling or attracting pests
miniChallenge: Look at your current trash and move one recyclable item to the right bin.
steps: Find the recycling and trash bins in your home.
Look up your local recycling rules online or on a printed guide.
Check an item you are unsure about against the guide.
Rinse out food or liquid from containers before placing them in recycling.
Place the item in the correct bin and make sure the lid closes.
completionCriteria: You checked your local recycling rules
You sorted at least one item correctly
commonProblems: You are not sure if an item is recyclable — check your local guide or look it up
The recycling bin smells — rinse containers and take the bin out more often
Your building has different rules — ask a manager or look for posted signs
tips: When in doubt, throw it out — putting wrong items in recycling can ruin a whole batch
Flatten cardboard boxes to save space
Keep a small bin near where you cook or eat to make sorting easier
